svg.text: Apartment 302 placement, scale, and read-distance board
A9 first-person carry-forward sheet: keep the corroborated nursery chain readable, the mirror bait tempting but wrong, and the false leads secondary under normal player viewing conditions.
Read bands
close inspect: 0.5m to 1.2m
mid corroboration: 1.2m to 2.4m
far attention pull: 2.4m to 4m
Rule: the nursery chain should still win the evidence argument at mid distance, even if the mirror lane steals attention briefly at far distance.
Corroborated nursery chain
These assets must start reading as one family from normal approach distance, not only after point-blank inspection.
Nursery floor sigil
`props/nursery_sigil_correct_ritual.svg`
Best read band: mid corroboration, still inspectable up close.
Placement: floor plane near the nursery route, angled to reveal halo rhythm from normal walk-up.
Scale caution: large enough to resolve as an anchor, not so large it becomes stage-floor spectacle.
Prayer-card support and nursery clue family
`props/prayer_cards_crown_circle.svg`, `references/hollow_saint_clue_language_board.svg`
Best read band: close inspect to mid corroboration.
Placement: side surfaces or nursery-adjacent furniture where repeated crown/halo logic can be compared against the sigil.
Scale caution: keep these smaller than the ritual anchor so corroboration feels cumulative, not louder than the floor mark.
Mirror bait and false leads
These assets are allowed to pull the eye first, but their placement and scale cannot let them survive the corroboration test.
Mirror-bait ritual
`props/mirror_circle_wrong_ritual.svg`
Best read band: far attention pull, then close inspection should reveal it as isolated and wrong.
Placement: off the nursery route or at a competing angle, never as the clean centerline destination of the room.
Scale caution: keep it impressive but not broader, brighter, or more repeated than the full nursery chain combined.
Mundane false leads
`props/broken_window_latch_false_lead.svg`, `props/pawn_ticket_pouch_false_lead.svg`
Best read band: close inspect, with only a light glance read at mid distance.
Placement: wall edge, side table, or floor-drop positions that support story noise rather than route-defining composition.
Scale caution: never cluster them into repeated spacing or halo-like rhythm near the nursery route.
First-person framing notes
Use this when deciding what should read from walk-up, what should reward inspection, and what should stay out of the main cone.
Floor marks: read best when the player can catch ellipse logic from a shallow forward angle, not only by looking straight down.
Wall-adjacent cues: keep latch, monitor, and similar support details near the edge of the frame so they invite inspection instead of replacing the route target.
Mirror bait: let it steal a glance from farther away, but avoid camera setups that center it longer or earlier than the nursery clue lane.
Prayer cards and paper clues: reward a step-in read. If the player can identify every card detail from across the room, they are oversized.
False-lead props: treat as side-channel story texture. If they begin reading like route anchors, reduce size, contrast, or composition privilege.
close inspect
mid corroboration
far attention
UI and prep-only handling
These items support readability and carry-forward work, but they are not room-placement targets by themselves.
UI-support surfaces
`ui/hollow_saint_clue_note_overlay.svg`, `ui/hollow_saint_ritual_status_overlay.svg` and A7 variants
Read condition: immediate screen-space support during inspection or ritual-read states, not environmental decoration.
Scale caution: protect the clue-note text field and center play cone before preserving ornament weight.
Prep and reference-only sheets
A5 export cards, A6 prep sheets, A8 contact sheet, and the earlier reference boards
Usage: open these before adaptation, but do not treat them as literal room-placed art or proof that runtime scale is already solved.
Main risk: a downstream pass can still flatten hierarchy by exporting the right art and placing it at the wrong scale or distance.
Quick-use rule: the mirror lane may win the first glance, but the nursery lane must win the walk-up read and the ritual decision.
